diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct bystander-observable sign of profound circulatory compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pelvic and shoulder pain preceding collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic bystander-reported symptom pattern for ruptured ectopic pregnancy with haemoperitoneum.
Establishes the likely pregnancy-related bleeding source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound hypotension, thready pulse, rigid distended abdomen with rebound, extreme pallor

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Haemorrhagic shock from ruptured ectopic pregnancy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation of massive haemoperitoneum; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Collapse with shoulder/pelvic pain and a history of amenorrhoea/bleeding together confirm ruptured ectopic pregnancy with haemorrhagic shock requiring maximal emergency response.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate Intensive Care Paramedic transport with pre-hospital IV fluid resuscitation
Emergency laparotomy/laparoscopy and blood transfusion on hospital arrival
Waiting for further history before positioning the patient or confirming ambulance dispatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Every minute of delay in a patient with haemorrhagic shock and semi-consciousness increases mortality risk.
